primary_argument: SMH is capturing broad-based semiconductor strength across the entire hardware ecosystem while outpacing QQQ (+2.97% vs +0.62%), signaling that the AI-driven CapEx cycle is accelerating in the physical layer faster than software can absorb it.
supporting_evidence: SMH +2.97% today on normal volume (0.9x 30-day avg) while QQQ +0.62%, demonstrating hardware/software rotation favoring semiconductors. NVDA +4.20% and TSM +1.39% anchoring the chain, with AVGO +5.66% showing downstream demand strength. The 4.8% VIX elevation to 16.9 remains in normal regime, eliminating fear-driven selling pressure that would otherwise cap upside.
key_assumption: Hyperscaler CapEx commitment to advanced AI infrastructure remains intact and continues to drive demand across the full semiconductor stack — from NVDA's GPUs through TSM's production capacity through AVGO's networking silicon — without disruption from export controls, geopolitical escalation, or demand destruction.
regime_sensitivity: In low-VIX (current regime at 16.9), SMH benefits from risk-on positioning and sustained institutional rotation into hardware. If VIX spikes above 20, SMH becomes vulnerable to growth repricing, but the current normal regime supports accumulation. A sustained VIX >25 would force reassessment of the CapEx durability assumption.
watch_items: SMH underperforms QQQ by >200bps over 5 trading days, signaling software/hardware rotation reversal and loss of AI CapEx acceleration momentum
NVDA drops >3% while SMH holds flat or gains, indicating NVDA-specific weakness that would reduce the top-20 component's drag on the ETF and suggest sector health intact
